item.g:title: Meter Works [Near MINT] Olympus 35 SP 35mm Film Camera 42mm f1.7 Lens from #253
item.g:description: Meter Works [Near MINT] Olympus 35 SP 35mm Film Camera 42mm f1.7 Lens from Japan #253 Description Discover the highly sought-after Olympus 35 SP, renowned for its excellent G.Zuiko 42mm f/1.7 lens and versatile spot/average metering system. This 35mm film camera is a fantastic choice for street photography and everyday shooting, offering superb image quality in a compact rangefinder package. Grade: Near Mint Appearance of the item Overall, the camera shows minimal signs of use. There are some minor scuffs on the body. A slight dent is noted next to the film advance lever. Please check the provided photos for detailed condition of the product. Functional We have thoroughly tested this camera, and it works properly. Inspection Results: Operation Checks: Shutter: Operates correctly at all speeds. Self-timer: Functions as expected. Light meter: Tested and confirmed to be working properly, responsive to light changes. AF: Manual focus (rangefinder patch) is clear and accurate. Helicoid: Focus ring turns smoothly without stickiness or excessive play. Aperture: Blades are clean, oil-free, and stop down correctly. Optics (Lens - G.Zuiko 42mm f/1.7): Dirt: None. Dust: Slight internal dust, typical for a camera of this age, which will not affect image quality. Haze: None. Fungus: Slight fungus is present on internal lens elements. While minimal, this has been disclosed upfront. Scratch: None. Optics (Viewfinder): Dirt: None. Dust: None. Haze: Slight haze is present, which does not significantly impede viewing but is noted. Fungus: None. Scratch: None. Common Known Issues for the Olympus 35 SP (Based on Real-Time Data): Based on extensive real-time data from other listings for this specific model, we highlight common issues and our findings for this unit: Built-in Exposure Meter (HIGH priority): This model is known for potential accuracy or responsiveness issues, even if appearing functional. Our unit's meter was tested and works properly, showing responsiveness to light. However, for critical applications, users may wish to verify readings against a known light source across various conditions. Internal Lens/Viewfinder Fungus (HIGH priority): Fungus can be present and degrade image quality. Our inspection found slight fungus on the internal lens elements (as noted above). The viewfinder is free of fungus. Film Advance Mechanism (MEDIUM priority): Issues with the film pressure plate or spring tension can affect film flatness or smooth advance. While our functional tests confirm smooth film advance, we have noted a slight dent near the film advance lever on this unit, which is cosmetic and does not impact mechanism operation. Internal Lens Barrel Mechanics (MEDIUM priority): Dried lubricants, internal haze, or significant dust can affect lens operation. Our tests confirmed the helicoid and aperture mechanisms are operating smoothly, and internal haze is absent in the lens. Molded Base Plate Integrity (MEDIUM priority): The original base plate can be prone to damage or cracking. We inspected the body and found it structurally sound, though general scuffs are present. The slight dent near the film advance lever is also a body integrity note. Leatherette Peeling (LOW priority): The body's leatherette can peel. On this unit, the leatherette is securely attached, with no peeling observed. This camera is NOT recommended for: Pristine Collectors: This unit has slight internal lens fungus, slight dust, slight viewfinder haze, and a minor body dent/scuffs, making it unsuitable for those seeking a cosmetically or optically flawless collector's item. Users Demanding Absolute Meter Precision Without Verification: While tested and working, the model's known tendency for meter variations means critical users may want to perform their own extended calibration checks. Buyers Unwilling to Accept Minor Optical Imperfections: The slight internal lens fungus and viewfinder haze, though minor, are present and disclosed. Included in this listing: Lens Cap If you have any question or concern, please do not hesitate to contact me. Thank you for visiting. For U.S. Buyers - DDP (Duties Prepaid / Included) Import duty and standard courier fees are included in the price/shipping cost. item.g:title: [Excellent 19,093 shots] Nikon D700 12.1MP Digital SLR Camera from Japan #303
item.g:description: [Excellent] Nikon D700 12.1MP Digital SLR Camera (19k Shots) #303 Description Product Overview This is a highly regarded Nikon D700, a full-frame 12.1MP DSLR known for its exceptional image quality and robust build. With a low shutter count of 19,093, it offers significant photographic potential. This particular unit has been thoroughly tested and is in proper working condition. Grade: Excellent Appearance of the item Some scuff marks as shown in photos. Please check the photos for detailed condition of the product. Functional Status Tested. Work properly. Specific Cosmetic/Optical Notes The rear LCD monitor has numerous spot mold/stains. While visible, it does not impede functionality for menu navigation or image review. The optical viewfinder surface shows coating peel-like stains. This is primarily cosmetic and does not significantly impact viewing or focusing. Detailed Inspection Results Operational Checks: Shutter: Confirmed OK across all speeds, including continuous shooting. Self-timer: Functions properly. Light Meter: Accurate readings confirmed. Flash: Built-in flash deploys and fires correctly. Hot shoe contacts appear clean. Shutter Count: 19,093 actuations. Optics (Viewfinder): Dirt: Slight dirt present. Dust: None. Haze: None. Fungus: None. Scratch: None. Other: Coating peel-like stains on the viewfinder surface. All tested functions are operating as expected. Important Notes: Known Issues for the Nikon D700 Model While our specific unit performed well in the tested areas, it's important for buyers to be aware of common vulnerabilities for the Nikon D700 model. Not all items below were specifically tested on this unit unless stated otherwise in "Detailed Inspection Results": Shutter Mechanism: Despite a low shutter count (this unit: 19,093), D700 shutters can sometimes fail prematurely due to manufacturing defects or component degradation. Our unit's shutter was confirmed working correctly at all speeds during inspection. Image Sensor Defects: Potential for dead/hot pixels or horizontal/vertical banding artifacts, particularly in long exposures or high ISO. We recommend thorough pixel peeping on test shots for critical users. External Connectivity Ports: Issues with USB for data transfer, HDMI for video output, or the 10-pin remote terminal. These ports were not individually tested on this unit, so functionality cannot be guaranteed. Controls & Dials: Physical buttons or command dials may become sticky, unresponsive, or erratic. All primary controls used during our functional check operated smoothly. Mirror Box Malfunction: The mirror box mechanism can malfunction, causing the mirror to stick or fail to return. Our inspection confirmed normal mirror movement during and after a shot. Autofocus System: Potential for inaccuracy, slow acquisition, or failure to lock focus. This unit's autofocus was responsive with our test lens, but extensive testing with various lenses or challenging conditions was not performed. Storage (CF Card Slot): CompactFlash card slot pins are susceptible to bending or breaking, or internal memory card controller circuitry can fail. We confirmed the camera recognizes and writes to a CF card without issue. Power Housing: Issues with the battery compartment (latch, contacts). This unit powers on and operates stably with a known good battery, implying the power housing is functional. Included Accessories None No other accessories (e.g., battery, charger, strap, body cap) are included unless explicitly mentioned. This Product is NOT Recommended For: Collectors or perfectionists: The presence of spot mold on the rear LCD and coating peel-like stains on the viewfinder means this unit is not cosmetically pristine. Users requiring flawlessly clear displays: While functional, the rear LCD's spot mold may be distracting for some. Users relying heavily on untested external ports: We did not individually test all connectivity ports (USB, HDMI, 10-pin remote terminal). Buyers unwilling to acknowledge model-specific risks: While tested functions are good, some D700 models can develop issues like sensor defects or AF system quirks over time, as detailed above. If you have any question or concern, please do not hesitate to contact me. Thank you for visiting. For U.S. Buyers - DDP (Duties Prepaid / Included) Import duty and standard courier fees are included in the price/shipping cost. item.g:title: [Excellent] Nikon COOLPIX A900 20.0MP Digital Camera Silver Zoom Lens #280
item.g:description: [Excellent] Nikon COOLPIX A900 20.0MP Digital Camera Silver Zoom Lens #280 Product Overview We are delighted to offer this Nikon COOLPIX A900, a versatile 20.0MP digital camera known for its powerful zoom lens and user-friendly interface. Ideal for capturing everyday moments and travel photography with ease, this camera combines portability with impressive image quality. Condition & Grade Grade: Excellent Appearance of the item This camera is in Excellent cosmetic condition overall. Weakness-First Disclosure - Noted Cosmetic Flaws: We observed a small dent on the lens frame. The rear LCD monitor exhibits numerous scuffs and scratches. Some general scuffing on the body. Please examine all photos carefully for precise cosmetic details. Functional Status We have thoroughly tested this camera, and it works properly. Detailed Inspection Results We performed comprehensive checks, specifically addressing common vulnerabilities of the Nikon COOLPIX A900 model: Main Controls & Buttons: All physical buttons (shutter, zoom, menu, etc.) and command dials respond accurately without stickiness or erratic behavior. Shutter Mechanism: Functions consistently across all speeds, including continuous shooting. No jams or incorrect exposure times observed. Autofocus (AF) System: Acquires focus quickly and accurately in various conditions. Built-in Flash: Deploys and fires reliably. Hot shoe contacts for external flashes were not tested. Rear LCD Display: While it has cosmetic scratches, the display functions perfectly with no dead pixels, lines, or reduced backlight brightness. Light Meter: Provides accurate readings. Self-timer: Functions correctly. Helicoid (Zoom Mechanism): Operates smoothly and responsively through its full range. Aperture: Opens and closes correctly; blades are clean. Overview Discover the charm of analog photography with this Olympus Trip 35, a classic 35mm point-and-shoot film camera known for its simplicity and excellent optics. This 'Excellent' grade camera is ideal for enthusiasts seeking a reliable vintage shooter. Detailed Inspection Results We conduct thorough inspections to ensure transparency and reliability. Operation Checks: Shutter: Operates correctly at all speeds. Light Meter: Responsive and accurate during testing, ensuring proper exposure. AF (Zone Focus): Smooth and functional, allowing for precise focusing. Helicoid: Moves smoothly without stiffness or play. Aperture: Blades are clean and move freely. Optics: Lens (Front & Internal Elements): Dirt: None Dust: Slight internal dust, not affecting image quality. Haze: None Fungus: None Scratch: None Viewfinder: Dirt: None Dust: None Haze: None Fungus: None Scratch: None Model-Specific Considerations for Olympus Trip 35 The Olympus Trip 35 is a beloved classic, but like all vintage cameras, it has common areas of wear. We've specifically checked for these: Light Meter (High Risk): While often prone to inaccuracy or unresponsiveness, our unit's light meter tested 'OK' and provided accurate readings across various lighting conditions. Note: As with any vintage meter, subtle variations might occur; we recommend verifying with your preferred film initially. Body Integrity / Damage (Medium Risk): The original molded base plate can be prone to damage. We noted a small dent on the bottom of the body (as detailed in Appearance). Beyond this, structural integrity around the tripod mount is sound. Film Advance (Medium/Critical Risk): The film advance and rewind mechanisms operate smoothly and correctly, indicating no issues with internal linkages, film flatness, or pressure plate tension. Optics Fungus (High Risk): Often found in vintage lenses, we are pleased to confirm 'None' for fungus in both the lens and viewfinder elements of this unit. Shutter (Critical Risk): Shutter actuation is consistent and responsive, confirming proper mechanical function. Lens Mechanics (Medium Risk): Focusing (helicoid) and aperture mechanisms operate smoothly without resistance or play. Optical clarity is excellent, with only slight dust. Cosmetic Body (Low Risk): Typical wear includes scuffs and minor dents, which are present as detailed in the 'Appearance' section, consistent with an 'Excellent' grade. Not Applicable: This model does not feature an electronic power system for core functions (like 'Power Failure' or 'Film Transport Motor issues') or an integrated flash unit, therefore these common issues are not relevant to the Olympus Trip 35. Included Accessories None This Product is NOT Recommended For Collectors requiring a cosmetically flawless, mint-condition example, due to minor scuffs and a small dent. Users who prefer fully automatic, modern digital cameras with advanced features. Those unwilling to occasionally verify light meter readings, as vintage meters, despite testing well, can sometimes have subtle variances. Aperture Blades: Blades are snappy, move freely, and close completely without stickiness or delay, despite a slight, non-impacting oil stain on some blades. Internal Lens Debris (Dust): A slight amount of internal dust is present. This is typical for the model and era and is minor enough not to cause flare, reduce contrast, or appear in images.
